report_name: US Agencies Warn of Ongoing Attacks Targeting Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) Systems

threat_actors.name: Iranian state-sponsored actors
threat_actors.description: Unattributed threat actors, with a potential link to Iranian state-sponsorship, are compromising internet-exposed Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) systems. The attribution is not formal.
threat_actors.goals: Disrupt critical infrastructure by disabling safety alerts on fuel monitoring systems, potentially causing physical and operational damage.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: Attackers are accessing and manipulating Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) systems through their exposed remote interfaces.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The compromise of ATG systems likely involves the use of default or easily guessable passwords to gain unauthorized access.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation of Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: Attackers gained access to manipulate ATG system functions, such as disabling alerts, by exploiting remote services.

attack_patterns.name: Impair Process Control
attack_patterns.description: By disabling system alerts on ATG systems, attackers directly impaired the process control function, which could lead to a loss of safety and undetected hazardous events like fuel spills.

identities.name: Energy Sector
identities.description: The energy sector was targeted through attacks on their Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) systems used for fuel monitoring.

identities.name: Chemical Sector
identities.description: The chemical sector was targeted through attacks on their Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) systems used for liquid monitoring.

identities.name: Food and Agriculture Sector
identities.description: The food and agriculture sector was targeted through attacks on their Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) systems.

identities.name: Transportation Sector
identities.description: The transportation sector was targeted through attacks on their Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) systems, disrupting fuel inventory tracking.

infrastructure.name: Internet-Exposed ATG System Interfaces
infrastructure.description: Internet-exposed web interfaces and serial ports of Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) systems, operating on ports 8001, 9001, and 10001, were exploited by attackers to gain remote access and execute commands.

indicators.name: Suspicious Inbound Traffic to ATG Ports
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Inbound Traffic to Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) Ports
id: 9c5b1a3d-8e7f-4b6c-9d1a-2b3c4d5e6f78
status: experimental
description: Detects inbound network connections to default ports used by Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) systems, which could indicate scanning or exploitation attempts.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa26-155a
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-04
tags:
  - attack.t0886
  - attack.initial_access
level: medium
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    dst_port:
      - 8001
      - 9001
      - 10001
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to default ports (8001, 9001, 10001) associated with ATG systems, as highlighted in the CISA/NSA advisory.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Inbound Traffic to ATG Ports
